title: B0: UART DMA Telemetry Logger
description:
**Scenario:** A battery-powered vibration monitor streams binary frames from a sensor hub to flash through UART.

**Task:** Implement a circular DMA receiver with idle-line packet detection and a safe handoff to the logging task.

**Given:**
- MCU clock is 72 MHz
- UART baud rate is 921600
- DMA buffer is 256 bytes
- Packets are 12-96 bytes with CRC-8

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- No byte-wise polling in the main loop
- No packet loss across two back-to-back frames
- CPU wakeups only on idle-line interrupt or buffer half/full events
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- 12, 64, and 96 byte packets are reconstructed exactly
- CRC failures are counted without corrupting the next packet
- CPU active time remains below 4% during continuous streaming

**Deliverables:**
- driver code
- state diagram
- one trace showing DMA pointer wraparound

test_cases.name: Check 1
test_cases.expectation: 12, 64, and 96 byte packets are reconstructed exactly

test_cases.name: Check 2
test_cases.expectation: CRC failures are counted without corrupting the next packet

test_cases.name: Check 3
test_cases.expectation: CPU active time remains below 4% during continuous streaming

interview_prompts: What assumption would you validate first on real hardware?
Which corner case is most likely to fail in production?
How would you prove the fix to a hiring panel?

title: B0: I2C Sensor Fault Recovery
description:
**Scenario:** An environmental sensing node occasionally locks up when a humidity sensor stretches SCL too long.

**Task:** Write a non-blocking I2C transaction manager that can recover a stuck bus and retry safely.

**Given:**
- 400 kHz I2C bus
- two sensors share the bus
- timeout threshold is 2 ms
- sensor address is 0x44

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Do not reset the whole MCU
- Recovery must pulse SCL exactly 9 times before STOP
- Retries must be bounded to 3 attempts
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- normal read returns temperature and humidity words
- stuck-SDA fault recovers within 5 ms
- NACK does not block the scheduler

**Deliverables:**
- transaction state machine
- bus recovery routine
- fault log format

test_cases.name: Check 1
test_cases.expectation: normal read returns temperature and humidity words

test_cases.name: Check 3
test_cases.expectation: NACK does not block the scheduler

interview_prompts: What assumption would you validate first on real hardware?
Which corner case is most likely to fail in production?
How would you prove the fix to a hiring panel?

title: B0: Low-Power Wakeup Scheduler
description:
**Scenario:** A wearable health patch must sample ECG for 20 ms every second and sleep the rest of the time.

**Task:** Design the timer, ADC, and sleep-mode sequence that meets the sampling schedule without drift.

**Given:**
- 32.768 kHz RTC
- ADC sample rate is 1 kS/s during active window
- radio upload happens every 60 samples

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Average current must stay below 180 uA
- Timestamp drift must be below 20 ms per hour
- ADC cannot run while the core is in deep sleep
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- active/sleep timeline is correct for 120 seconds
- missed wakeups are detected
- radio upload does not disturb the next sample window

**Deliverables:**
- timing diagram
- scheduler pseudocode
- power budget table

test_cases.name: Check 1
test_cases.expectation: active/sleep timeline is correct for 120 seconds

test_cases.name: Check 3
test_cases.expectation: radio upload does not disturb the next sample window

interview_prompts: What assumption would you validate first on real hardware?
Which corner case is most likely to fail in production?
How would you prove the fix to a hiring panel?

title: B0: CAN Bus Safety Node
description:
**Scenario:** An automotive actuator receives torque-limit commands on CAN and must fail safe on message loss.

**Task:** Implement message validation, timeout handling, and a degraded-mode state machine.

**Given:**
- 500 kbps CAN
- message period is 10 ms
- timeout limit is 35 ms
- payload includes counter and CRC nibble

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Reject repeated counters
- Enter safe torque mode on timeout
- Recover only after 5 consecutive valid frames
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- counter rollover from 15 to 0 is accepted
- single corrupt frame is ignored
- three missed frames trigger safe state

**Deliverables:**
- state table
- C validation function
- test vector table

test_cases.name: Check 1
test_cases.expectation: counter rollover from 15 to 0 is accepted

test_cases.name: Check 3
test_cases.expectation: three missed frames trigger safe state

interview_prompts: What assumption would you validate first on real hardware?
Which corner case is most likely to fail in production?
How would you prove the fix to a hiring panel?

title: B0: Pipelined 4-Tap FIR Core
description:
**Scenario:** A baseband accelerator needs one filtered sample every clock after pipeline fill.

**Task:** Write synthesizable RTL for a signed 4-tap FIR filter with valid/ready flow control.

**Given:**
- input is signed Q1.15
- coefficients are signed Q1.15
- output is rounded Q2.14
- clock target is 150 MHz

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- No combinational multiplier chain longer than one multiplier
- Reset must flush valid bits
- Overflow must saturate, not wrap
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- impulse response matches coefficients
- step input saturates correctly
- backpressure holds all pipeline stages

**Deliverables:**
- RTL module
- testbench vectors
- latency and resource explanation
interview_prompts: What assumption would you validate first on real hardware?
Which corner case is most likely to fail in production?
How would you prove the fix to a hiring panel?

title: B0: Asynchronous FIFO CDC
description:
**Scenario:** A camera interface crosses pixels from a 96 MHz sensor clock into a 125 MHz processing clock.

**Task:** Design an async FIFO using Gray-coded pointers and two-flop synchronizers.

**Given:**
- FIFO depth is 16
- data width is 12 bits
- read clock may pause for up to 8 cycles

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Full and empty flags must be glitch-free
- Binary pointers must not cross domains directly
- Reset can deassert asynchronously
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- no data reordering over 1000 random writes/reads
- full prevents overwrite
- empty prevents underflow

**Deliverables:**
- RTL
- CDC explanation
- randomized testbench

test_cases.name: Check 1
test_cases.expectation: no data reordering over 1000 random writes/reads

title: B0: AXI-Lite Register Block
description:
**Scenario:** A verification interview asks you to build a small memory-mapped control block.

**Task:** Create an AXI-Lite compatible register block with status, control, and clear-on-write interrupt bits.

**Given:**
- 4 registers, 32-bit each
- byte strobes are supported
- read latency may be 1 cycle

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Writes must honor WSTRB
- interrupt status clears only when writing 1
- invalid addresses return SLVERR
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- unaligned address is rejected
- partial byte writes update only selected lanes
- clear-on-write does not clear unrelated bits

**Deliverables:**
- RTL
- protocol timing notes
- directed tests

title: B0: Anti-Alias RC Filter
description:
**Scenario:** A 12-bit ADC samples a 0-2 kHz sensor signal at 10 kS/s in a noisy motor controller.

**Task:** Choose RC values, estimate cutoff, and verify attenuation at the PWM noise frequency.

**Given:**
- signal bandwidth is 2 kHz
- PWM noise is at 40 kHz
- ADC input capacitance is 8 pF
- source resistance should be below 10 kOhm

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Use E24 resistor values
- loading error below 0.5%
- attenuation at 40 kHz at least 22 dB
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- cutoff is between 2.5 and 4 kHz
- settling error before ADC sample is below 0.5 LSB
- component tolerance impact is explained

**Deliverables:**
- calculation table
- SPICE netlist
- Bode plot interpretation

title: B0: Buck Converter Feedback Divider
description:
**Scenario:** A 5 V to 1.8 V buck regulator powers an RF transceiver with strict standby current.

**Task:** Select the feedback divider and feed-forward capacitor for stable regulation and low quiescent loss.

**Given:**
- reference voltage is 0.6 V
- switching frequency is 1.2 MHz
- feedback pin leakage is 50 nA max

title: B0: Sequence Detector 1011
description:
**Scenario:** A serial protocol detector must identify overlapping 1011 patterns in a bitstream.

**Task:** Design Moore and Mealy FSM versions and compare latency/output behavior.

**Given:**
- one bit arrives per clock
- patterns may overlap
- reset is synchronous active high

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- No missed overlap cases
- state encoding must be documented
- Mealy output must be glitch-safe at register boundary
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- 1011011 produces two detections
- all-zero stream produces none
- reset mid-pattern returns to idle

**Deliverables:**
- state diagram
- RTL
- test vector table

title: B0: Hamming SECDED Encoder
description:
**Scenario:** A memory controller needs single-error correction and double-error detection for 8-bit data.

**Task:** Implement parity generation and syndrome decoding for SECDED.

**Given:**
- data width is 8 bits
- codeword includes parity bits plus overall parity
- single-bit error location must be reported

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Correct one flipped bit
- Flag but do not correct double-bit errors
- No latches inferred
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- all single-bit positions correct over exhaustive test
- double-bit fault raises uncorrectable flag
- clean codeword passes unchanged

**Deliverables:**
- encoder/decoder RTL
- syndrome table
- exhaustive testbench

- flag behavior matches operation result

title: B0: Two-Layer Sensor Board Layout
description:
**Scenario:** A compact sensor board mixes an MCU, I2C sensor, LDO, USB connector, and status LED.

**Task:** Create placement and routing rules that pass manufacturability and reduce noise coupling.

**Given:**
- board size is 35 mm x 25 mm
- 2-layer FR4
- minimum trace/space is 6/6 mil
- sensor sits near board edge

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Keep analog sensor traces away from USB D+/D-
- place decoupling within 2 mm of power pins
- maintain one continuous ground return where possible
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- all power pins have local decoupling
- no high-speed trace crosses split return path
- test points exist for 3V3, GND, SDA, SCL, UART TX/RX

**Deliverables:**
- placement screenshot notes
- routing checklist
- BOM risk notes

title: B0: Secure OTA Update Flow
description:
**Scenario:** An IoT gateway must update firmware safely over an unreliable network.

**Task:** Design download, verification, swap, rollback, and version checks for OTA.

**Given:**
- dual-bank flash
- firmware image has SHA-256 and ECDSA signature
- power can fail during download

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- Never boot unsigned image
- rollback after two failed boots
- version downgrade blocked unless factory override is set
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- power loss during download keeps old firmware
- bad signature is rejected
- new firmware commits only after health check

**Deliverables:**
- state diagram
- metadata format
- failure-mode table

test_cases.name: Check 1
test_cases.expectation: power loss during download keeps old firmware

test_cases.name: Check 3
test_cases.expectation: new firmware commits only after health check

Which corner case is most likely to fail in production?
How would you prove the fix to a hiring panel?

title: B0: Edge Anomaly Detector
description:
**Scenario:** A pump-monitoring node must detect abnormal vibration locally before uploading a summary.

**Task:** Compute lightweight features and threshold logic suitable for an MCU.

**Given:**
- accelerometer sample rate is 1 kHz
- window length is 256 samples
- features are RMS, peak, crest factor

**Difficulty Focus:** build the core idea correctly and explain it cleanly.

**Constraints:**
- RAM use below 4 KB
- no dynamic allocation
- false alarm rate must be tunable
- Keep the implementation minimal but correct.
- Write down the assumptions you made.

**Expected Checks:**
- normal sine window stays below threshold
- impact spike raises anomaly flag
- feature values match reference calculations

**Deliverables:**
- feature extraction code
- threshold tuning notes
- payload schema

test_cases.name: Check 1
test_cases.expectation: normal sine window stays below threshold
